entries.title: Sydney will become Anthropic’s fourth office in Asia-Pacific
entries.summary: Anthropic is expanding into Australia and New Zealand by opening an office in Sydney, its fourth in the Asia-Pacific region. This move is driven by the demand from local businesses and aims to enhance collaborations with Australian institutions, focusing on areas like AI applications and national priorities.

entries.title: Anthropic invests $100 million into the Claude Partner Network
entries.summary: Anthropic is launching the Claude Partner Network, committing an initial $100 million to support partners that help enterprises adopt Claude. This initiative will provide training, technical support, and joint market development, ensuring that partners are equipped to facilitate successful AI deployments.

entries.title: Introducing The Anthropic Institute
entries.summary: The Anthropic Institute is being launched to tackle the significant challenges posed by powerful AI systems. It aims to provide insights to researchers and the public while fostering partnerships to mitigate the risks associated with advancing AI technology.

entries.title: Leanstral: Open-Source foundation for trustworthy vibe-coding
entries.summary: Mistral AI introduces Leanstral, the first open-source code agent for Lean 4, designed to improve efficiency in formal proofs and software specifications. This innovative tool allows users to dictate their needs while Leanstral effectively carries out coding tasks and formally verifies implementations.

entries.title: Mistral AI partners with NVIDIA to accelerate open frontier models
entries.summary: Mistral AI has joined the NVIDIA Nemotron Coalition as a founding member to enhance large-scale model development and multimodal capabilities. This partnership aims to co-develop open-source AI models, leveraging both companies' technologies for a collaborative and accessible future in artificial intelligence.

entries.title: Mapping the World's Forests with Greater Precision: Introducing Canopy Height Maps v2
entries.summary: Meta, in partnership with the World Resources Institute, has launched Canopy Height Maps v2 (CHMv2), a global open-source model designed to enhance forest mapping and biodiversity efforts. By leveraging advanced AI technology, CHMv2 aims to provide higher accuracy in forest measurements, crucial for environmental monitoring and conservation strategies.

entries.title: Ensemble and Cohere building the first RCM‑native healthcare LLM
entries.summary: This article discusses the collaboration between Ensemble and Cohere to create the first healthcare language model specifically designed for revenue cycle management (RCM). It highlights the innovative approaches being implemented to enhance healthcare services through advanced AI technologies.

entries.title: Ensemble and Cohere building the first RCM‑native healthcare LLM
entries.summary: Cohere and Ensemble are collaborating to develop the first revenue cycle management (RCM)-native large language model for healthcare. This custom model addresses the complexities of the healthcare payments ecosystem by incorporating operational insights and expertise, aiming to improve accuracy and reduce overhead in revenue cycle management processes.

entries.title: Introducing Perplexity Health
entries.summary: Perplexity Health is launched to provide accurate health information by connecting personal health data from various sources. It offers a personalized dashboard to answer health questions by integrating lab results, medical records, and wearable data.

entries.title: Announcing the Secure Intelligence Institute
entries.summary: Perplexity is launching the Secure Intelligence Institute (SII), a flagship research center focused on advancing security, privacy, and trust in frontier AI. This initiative builds on notable previous research and engineering work to address security questions in AI systems and foster collaboration across academia and industry.

entries.title: Introducing the Perplexity Health Advisory Board
entries.summary: Perplexity announces the creation of the Health Advisory Board, consisting of physicians, researchers, and health technology leaders, to enhance the quality of health information and support patient-clinician interactions. This initiative aims to ensure that future product decisions reflect evidence-based medicine and clinical rigor.
